summary: Implementation plan for adding currentEmpCode based on NIK without removing parsedCode. This feature enables tracking employee code changes over time using NIK as the stable identity key from db_ptrj.HR_EMPLOYEE.
approach: Phased implementation starting with database migrations, followed by HR snapshot sync script, backend service updates for currentEmpCode resolution, API updates, backfill scripts, frontend updates, and finally quality checks. The implementation preserves parsedCode as historical record while adding currentEmpCode for final absensi mapping.

phases.name: Database & Migrations
phases.description: Create all required database schema changes including new columns, tables, and indexes

phases.name: HR Snapshot Sync Script
phases.description: Create script to sync hr_employee_current_snapshot from db_ptrj.HR_EMPLOYEE

phases.name: Backend Services (currentEmpCode Resolution)
phases.description: Update employee-mapping, sync-orchestrator, and attendance-process services

phases.name: API Updates
phases.description: Update attendance and employees API routes for currentEmpCode display

phases.name: Backfill Scripts
phases.description: Create and run backfill scripts for existing data

_metadata.prd: PRD: Penambahan currentEmpCode Berbasis NIK tanpa Menghapus parsedCode
audit_fixes_applied: P0: employees table schema verified - nik, hr_employee_code, hr_loc_code, hr_status, hr_verified, hr_verified_at already exist (migration 042)
P0: IMPL-006 adjusted - only add: current_emp_code, current_emp_name, identity_source, identity_resolution_reason
P0: attendance_imports.parsed_employee_code clarified - intentional audit trail duplicate
P1: db_ptrj server will use env variable: process.env.HR_DB_SERVER
_metadata.hr_resolution_rule: ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY LTRIM(RTRIM(NewICNo)) ORDER BY CASE WHEN Status='1' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, UpdateDate DESC, CreateDate DESC, EmpCode DESC)

design_decisions.decision: Use NIK as stable identity key
design_decisions.rationale: NIK (NewICNo) from db_ptrj.HR_EMPLOYEE is the only stable identifier that persists across employee code changes
design_decisions.tradeoff: Requires additional join to HR table but ensures accurate historical tracking

design_decisions.rationale: parsedCode shows what employee code was used at the time of scanning, critical for audit trail
design_decisions.tradeoff: Additional storage overhead but maintains data integrity

design_decisions.decision: Use snapshot table for current employee state
design_decisions.rationale: Performance optimization - avoid re-computing ROW_NUMBER() on every sync
design_decisions.tradeoff: Requires periodic refresh of snapshot table

design_decisions.rationale: Clear precedence rules for mapping resolution with fallback chain
design_decisions.tradeoff: More complex mapping logic but comprehensive coverage

data_flow.diagram: raw_device_user_id -> parsedCode (SSOT Parser) -> NIK (HR Lookup) -> currentEmpCode (Snapshot Lookup) -> attendance_imports
